metadata.title: Exploring the Therapeutic Potential of Sericin in Tissue Regeneration and Wound Healing
metadata.description: Dev, Dr. Poonam, Mukaram, Maneeza, Fatima, Umaisa, Abstract, Zuha Ibtisam (2025): Exploring the Therapeutic Potential of Sericin in Tissue Regeneration and Wound Healing. FishTaxa 37 (9): 244-254
references: 1. P. Aramwit, N. Bang, J. Ratanavaraporn, and S. Ekgasit, "Green synthesis of silk sericin- capped silver nanoparticles and their potent antibacterial activity," Nanoscale Research Letters, vol. 8, no. 1, p. 579, 2013. doi: 10.1186/1556-276X-8-579.

2. P. Aramwit, N. Bang, T. Siritientong, and W. Sungkarat, "Properties and cytotoxicity of sericin- polyvinyl alcohol gel for wound dressing," Biomedical Materials, vol. 7, no. 3, p. 035008, 2012. doi: 10.1088/1748-6041/7/3/035008.

3. P. Aramwit, S. Kanokpanont, W. De-Eknamkul, and T. Srichana, "The effect of sericin from various extraction methods on cell viability and collagen production," International Journal of Molecular Sciences, vol. 11, no. 5, pp. 2200-2211, 2010. doi: 10.3390/ijms11052200.

4. P. Aramwit, P. Towiwat, and T. Srichana, "Anti- inflammatory effects of silk sericin: Potential therapeutic application in a murine model of allergic contact dermatitis," Biological & Pharmaceutical Bulletin, vol. 35, no. 3, pp. 377-382, 2012. doi: 10.1248/bpb.35.377.

5. E. Bari, M. Sorlini, G. Marrubini, M. L. Torre, and S. Perteghella, "Sericin and crocetin- based bioactive nanoparticles for intervertebral disc regeneration: An in vitro feasibility study," Colloids and Surfaces B: Biointerfaces, vol. 225, p. 113199, 2023. doi: 10.1016/j.colsurfb.2023.113199.

6. B. Biman, N. Biswas, and S. K. Das, "Silk sericin- based self-assembled nanomicelles for dual drug delivery: Formulation, characterization, and cellular uptake," Journal of Biomedical Nanotechnology, vol. 5, no. 4, pp. 367-375, 2009. doi: 10.1166/jbn.2009.1071.

7. Z. Chen et al., "3D- printed sericin/GelMA hydrogel promotes wound healing through regulating epidermal growth factor," Journal of Biomaterials Science, Polymer Edition, vol. 29, no. 18, pp. 2151-2167, 2018. doi: 10.1080/09205063.2018.1522195.

8. B. C. Dash, B. B. Mandal, and S. C. Kundu, "Silk fibroin and silk sericin protein- based matrices for wound healing and drug delivery," Biopolymers, vol. 89, no. 9, pp. 823-835, 2008. doi: 10.1002/bip.21033.

9. R. Dash, C. Acharya, D. L. Kaplan, and S. C. Kundu, "Silk protein- based hydrogels for tissue engineering and regenerative medicine," Biotechnology and Bioengineering, vol. 117, no. 3, pp. 953-967, 2020. doi: 10.1002/bit.27250.
10. S. Guo and L. A. DiPietro, "Factors affecting wound healing," Journal of Dental Research, vol. 89, no. 3, pp. 219-229, 2010. doi: 10.1177/0022034509359125.

11. N. Kato et al., "Silk protein, sericin, suppresses the production of proinflammatory cytokines by lipopolysaccharide- stimulated macrophages," Bioscience, Biotechnology, and Biochemistry, vol. 69, no. 2, pp. 403-408, 2005. doi: 10.1271/bbb.69.403.

12. B. Kundu, R. Rajkhowa, S. C. Kundu, and X. Wang, "Silk fibroin biomaterials for tissue regenerations," Advanced Drug Delivery Reviews, vol. 65, no. 4, pp. 457-470, 2013. doi: 10.1016/j.addr.2012.09.043.

13. B. B. Mandal, N. Bhardwaj, and S. C. Kundu, "Injectable silk sericin- based hydrogel for immunoprotective pancreatic islet cell delivery," ACS Biomaterials Science & Engineering, vol. 7, no. 10, pp. 4875-4887, 2021. doi: 10.1021/acsbiomaterials.1c00410.
14. F. J. O'Brien, "Biomaterials & scaffolds for tissue engineering," Materials Today, vol. 14, no. 3, pp. 88-95, 2011. doi: 10.1016/S1369-7021(11)70058-X.
15. M. N. Padamwar and A. P. Pawar, "Silk sericin and its applications: A review," Journal of Scientific and Industrial Research, vol. 63, no. 4, pp. 323-329, 2004.

metadata.title: KAM Tori Seismic Periodicity and H³ Fault Network Geodesics for Earthquake Prediction Certificates
metadata.description: Tectonic fault systems are near-integrable Hamiltonians. OmegaFlow v13 KAMCheck() provides seismic stability certificate — KAM failure signals transition to chaotic regime (large ruptures). Theorem OG-GEO01: Lyapunov λ from H³ seismic waveform trajectory distinguishes stable creep (λ<0.001) from nucleating rupture (λ>0.01) providing 5-30 second early warning. Selberg zeta Z(s,Γ) for fault network counts closed rupture paths — formal catalog of resonant seismic cycles. κ=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: KAM Tori Seismic Periodicity and H³ Fault Network Geodesics for Earthquake Prediction Certificates
